news.headline: Church & Dwight to discuss Q1 2025 earnings on May 1 at 10:00 a.m. ET. Webcast accessible at https://investor.churchdwight.com/. Founded in 1846, Church & Dwight is a major U.S. producer of baking soda.
news.summary: Church & Dwight Co., Inc. (NYSE: CHD) will host a webcast to discuss its first quarter earnings on Thursday, May 1, 2025, at 10:00 a.m. ET. The company, established in 1846, is a major U.S. manufacturer of sodium bicarbonate, commonly referred to as baking soda. Investors and media can tune into the live audio webcast at https://investor.churchdwight.com/ from 10:00 a.m. ET, with a replay of the webcast available later.

news.headline: Podcast explores origins of Trump’s global trade war and its impacts worldwide.
Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister discussed situation with negotiation process on Iran's nuclear program with Russia's Ryabkov - RIA.
Santander US reports increase in auto demand post-pandemic, with 47% considering car purchase in the next year.
news.summary: Santander Holdings USA, Inc. revealed new survey results indicating a surge in pent-up auto demand among middle-income Americans. The data shows that 42% have increased their frequency of driving to work in the past year. Despite 52% postponing vehicle purchases in the last 12 months due to financial reasons, 47% are now contemplating buying a vehicle in the coming year, which is an increase from 41% in mid-2023. This rise in potential buyers reflects a growing trend towards considering auto purchases among middle-income earners returning to work.

news.headline: Trump said Musk will step back from current role in coming weeks - Politico
Waton Financial Limited closed IPO with 4,375,000 ordinary shares at $4.00 per share, raising $17.5 million in gross proceeds.
news.summary: On April 02, 2025, Waton Financial Limited (WTF), a holding company based in the British Virgin Islands, completed its initial public offering of 4,375,000 ordinary shares at $4.00 per share, resulting in gross proceeds of $17,500,000. Through its subsidiaries, Waton Securities International Limited and Waton Technology International Limited in Hong Kong, WTF offers securities brokerage and financial technology services. The Ordinary Shares started trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the ticker symbol “WTF” on April 1, 2025.

news.headline: Federal Register: US Commerce Department revises aluminum duties to add beer and empty aluminum cans.
Trump tells inner circle that Musk will leave soon - Politico

Industry faced vulnerabilities post $1.5B Bybit hack but $40M funds frozen by Chainalysis, helping drive forward critical insights in 2025 trends and regulatory directions. AI plays dual role in security, while blockchain transparency aids in fighting back. Levin stresses importance of combining Web2 and Web3 best practices in safeguarding against future cyber threats. DEXs remain accountable despite anonymity. Bybit, industry solidarity key in fostering mutual support and resilience.
news.summary: The article discusses the aftermath of the $1.5 billion Bybit hack, the largest crypto theft to date, and the increased vulnerabilities faced by the crypto industry. Following the hack, over $40 million of stolen funds were frozen, with Chainalysis playing a significant role. Chainalysis's reports, especially the annual Crypto Crime Report, have become essential resources for regulators and investigators. The report suggests that illicit cryptocurrency volume is expected to exceed 2023 levels, with ransomware and darknet markets making a comeback. Jonathan Levin, Chainalysis Co-Founder and CEO, highlighted the importance of real-time response to hacks, the use of AI in cybersecurity, and combing Web2 and Web3 best practices. Despite the severity of the Bybit attack, Levin emphasized the role of blockchain transparency in fighting back and how collaborative efforts could strengthen defenses against cyberattacks. Levin also discussed the accountability of decentralized exchanges in managing risks and curbing illicit activities. The article concludes by emphasizing the need for the crypto community to unite resources and intelligence to create a more secure digital financial environment.

news.headline: Publix ranked No. 42 on Fortune's 100 Best Companies to Work For list for the 28th consecutive year. CEO Kevin Murphy pleased.
news.summary: A company based in Lakeland, Florida, Publix, has been ranked No. 42 on Fortune's 100 Best Companies to Work For list for the 28th year in a row. This makes Publix one of only four companies to have made the list every year since it began in 1998. Publix's CEO, Kevin Murphy, emphasized that part of the company's mission is to prioritize the dignity, value, and job security of its employees. This recognition for Publix reflects its commitment to these values and principles, as highlighted by their consistent presence on the prestigious list.

news.headline: TOP WIN INTERNATIONAL LTD priced IPO at $4.00/share on Nasdaq, offering 2,664,000 Ordinary Shares. specializes in luxury watches.
news.summary: Today in Hong Kong, TOP WIN INTERNATIONAL LIMITED (Nasdaq: TOPW), a company focused on trading luxury watches, disclosed the pricing for its initial public offering. The offering consists of 2,664,000 ordinary shares being sold at a rate of $4.00 per share. TOP WIN is a Hong Kong-based wholesaler known for its expertise in the distribution and retail of luxury watches from global brands.

news.headline: Bruce Ely named Interim Chair of State Tax Advisory Board at Bloomberg Tax & Accounting, succeeding Art Rosen.
news.summary: In Arlington, Virginia on April 2, 2025, Bloomberg Tax & Accounting disclosed the selection of Bruce Ely as the new Interim Chair of the State Tax Advisory Board. Ely, a highly respected tax expert, takes over from Art Rosen of McDermott Will & Emery LLP. Ely brings a wealth of experience to the role, having worked extensively in the tax field. His appointment follows the tenure of Rosen, who previously held the position.

news.headline: Trinity Education Group (TEG) marks 18th anniversary in EdTech, empowering educators nationwide. Celebrating nearly two decades of impact.
news.summary: In Clarksville, Maryland, on April 2, 2025, Trinity Education Group (TEG) is celebrating its 18th anniversary. TEG is renowned for its cutting-edge educational technology solutions that empower educators nationwide. Over the past 18 years, TEG has made a significant impact in the education sector, with a focus on leveraging technology to advance learning. Through its initiatives, TEG has influenced countless educators and students, contributing to the enhancement of education across the country. This milestone commemorates nearly two decades of success and innovation in the field of education technology.

news.headline: Brad Hill appointed CEO & Corey Robertson COO of P.F. Chang's. Hill replaces Eduardo Luz. Luz remains as advisor till April.
news.summary: The Chief Operating Officer, Brad Hill, has been appointed as the new Chief Executive Officer of P.F. Chang's following the departure of Eduardo Luz. Luz will stay on as an advisor until April. The announcement was made on April 2, 2025 in Scottsdale, Arizona. This transition marks a significant change in leadership for the company.

news.headline: US Decision Intelligence Market to grow to $11.0 billion by 2030, from $3.0 billion in 2024, at a 23.5% CAGR.
news.summary: In Delray Beach, Florida, as of April 2, 2025, the market for Decision Intelligence in the United States is projected to see substantial growth, with expectations to reach a value of USD 11.0 billion by 2030. This marks a significant increase from the recorded USD 3.0 billion in 2024. The compound annual growth rate (CAGR) for this market segment during the forecast period is estimated at 23.5%. These findings are based on a recent report by MarketsandMarkets™. The analysis highlights the prominence of Decision Intelligence in driving advancements and strategic decision-making processes within the market.

news.headline: Inventor from Maine creates BIRD BOX for bird care/watching, providing a new way to observe and care for wild birds.
news.summary: In Pittsburgh on April 2, 2025, an inventor from Auburn, Maine shared their motivation for creating a new product for observing and caring for wild birds. The inventor expressed a desire to incorporate birds into daily life through a unique invention known as the BIRD BOX. This innovation aims to provide a fun and calming experience while birds freely come and go. The inventor's vision is to offer a way to connect with nature and appreciate the beauty of wildlife, showcasing their passion for creating a product that can enhance the interaction with wild birds.

news.headline: Goldman Sachs lowers S&P 500 forecast due to weaker growth and higher tariffs, predicting 5% drop in 3 months.
news.summary: Goldman Sachs has updated its projections for the S&P 500 index, painting a gloomy picture for investors. Chief equity strategist David J. Kostin announced a revised year-end target for the index, anticipating a 5% decline within the next three months compared to earlier estimates of no change. The 12-month price target for the S&P 500 has been lowered to 5,900 points, showing a modest 6% increase from current levels, a significant drop from the previous projection of a 16% gain. These adjustments are driven by a less optimistic outlook on the economy, with GDP growth predicted to slow to 1.5% annually, core personal consumption expenditures inflation expected to rise to 3.5%, and U.S. tariffs forecasted to reach 18% - a considerable increase from current levels. Reflecting the volatile market conditions, the S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSE:SPY) has already experienced a more than 5% dip year-to-date.

news.headline: Equity markets saw downside last week, with S&P 500 down 1.97%, Nasdaq 100 down 2.61%, and Dow Jones down 1.69%. Institutional outflows reached $20.3B indicating market repositioning. Tech sector experienced a 20% drawdown, while consumer sentiment dropped significantly. Fixed income saw flight to quality with T-note futures up. Gold reached $3,116 per ounce. US trade policy caused declines in Euro markets. Sector rotation favored defensive stocks and tech sector faces capacity utilization challenges. Key upcoming events include non-farm payroll report and GDP estimates. Tactical precision with strategic positioning recommended for portfolio construction.
news.summary: The article discusses the market performance and technical indicators for the week ending March 31 - April 4, 2025. The S&P 500 dropped by 1.97%, the Dow Jones Industrials shed 1.69%, and the Nasdaq 100 experienced a 2.61% decline. The article also highlights the recent trend of growth stocks underperforming value stocks, with large-cap growth down 10.0% YTD. The primary catalyst driving risk repricing has been the administration's tariff implementations, leading to significant market volatility. Additionally, various economic indicators point towards stagflationary signals, with inflation exceeding expectations and consumer sentiment deteriorating. The Federal Reserve is facing internal debate on policy calibration to manage inflation and support growth. The article also includes analysis on various market sectors, commodity markets, international market correlations, as well as forward catalysts and portfolio construction implications. Overall, it emphasizes the need for strategic positioning and multi-asset diversification amidst current market volatility.

news.headline: NASA faces implications by erasing 'First Woman' on Moon; source.com.
news.summary: A recent report reveals that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to a record low of 3.5%. This is a significant decrease from the previous rate of 4.2%. Additionally, the economy added a remarkable 250,000 new jobs last month, surpassing expectations. The positive job market trends have led to increased consumer confidence and spending, boosting overall economic growth. Experts predict that the labor market will continue to strengthen in the coming months, further driving down the unemployment rate.

news.headline: Eni in talks for Plenitude valuation of €13 billion.

Gold outperformed S&P 500 in March- gold rose by 9%, widest margin since 2011, while S&P 500 tumbled 7.6%. Gold ETF saw $2.8B inflows, while S&P 500 ETF saw $18B outflows. Trade war fears led to shift to safe havens.
news.summary: Gold had a remarkable performance in March, outshining U.S. equities by the widest margin in 13 years. The bullion is set to beat the S&P 500 by nearly 18 percentage points, with gold prices surging by 9%, the largest monthly increase since July 2020. Throughout the month, gold hit multiple all-time highs. In contrast, the S&P 500 saw a significant decline of 7.6%. This marked the strongest one-month leap for gold over U.S. equities since August 2011. Investor sentiment followed suit, with the SPDR Gold Shares ETF (NYSE: GLD) attracting $2.8 billion in March, adding to the $4 billion from February. On the other hand, the SPDR S&P 500 ETF (NYSE: SPY) experienced massive outflows of $18 billion, the largest since January.

news.headline: Judge permanently dismisses corruption case against NYC Mayor Adams, from Bloomberg.
Disclosure: Form 8.3 reports on Primary Health Properties Plc, disclosing essential details without specifying the source.
Tesla's stock dropped as quarterly vehicle deliveries may hit 8.25 million, the lowest in over two years.
news.summary: On Monday, Tesla's stock experienced a decline as investors awaited the upcoming quarterly vehicle delivery report scheduled for Wednesday. Analysts suspect that the upcoming data might reveal the lowest quarterly deliveries for Tesla in over two years. This news has raised concerns among investors and stakeholders, signaling a potential downturn in Tesla's performance. The anticipation surrounding the quarterly report has significantly impacted Tesla's stock value, indicating the importance of the upcoming figures to the market.

news.headline: FDA approves Sanofi's Qfitlia for hemophilia, 1st AT therapy for prophylaxis in patients aged 12+, based on ATLAS phase 3 data demonstrating reduced bleeding episodes.
news.summary: Sanofi SA's Qfitlia (fitusiran) has been approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration as the first antithrombin-lowering therapy for prophylaxis against bleeding episodes in adult and pediatric patients (aged 12 or older) with hemophilia A or B, with or without factor VIII or IX inhibitors. The approval follows data from the ATLAS phase 3 studies showing significant reduction in annualized bleeding rates. By reducing antithrombin levels, Qfitlia boosts thrombin generation to restore hemostasis. This breakthrough marks an important advancement in the treatment of hemophilia and represents a significant milestone in Sanofi's efforts to enhance their immune system-focused pipeline.

news.headline: Vaxcyte drops developing a pneumococcal conjugate vaccine, focusing on VAX-24 after Phase 2 study. VAX-31 Phase 3 in 2026.
news.summary: Vaxcyte Inc. presented findings from its Phase 2 trial of VAX-24, a 24-valent pneumococcal vaccine, versus Pfizer Inc’s Prevnar 20 in babies. The Mid-dose of VAX-24 was chosen for further development towards a Phase 3 program. Results from the VAX-31 infant Phase 2 study are expected in mid-2026, after which a Phase 3 study with either VAX-24 or VAX-31 will commence. The Phase 2 trial showed VAX-24 to be safe and well-tolerated. For more details, consult Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Dow Jones drops 400 points due to Trump tariff threat; Nvidia, Palantir, Tesla also dive sharply.
news.summary: On account of President Donald Trump's impending tariffs, the Dow Jones plummeted by 400 points causing significant sell-offs for Nvidia, Palantir, and Tesla. The impact was felt across the market as these companies experienced sharp declines in their stock values. The threat of tariffs imposed by President Trump contributed to this downturn, leading to a substantial decrease in the Dow Jones and affecting the performance of key technology companies.

news.headline: Novo Nordisk presents data on Phase 3b STRIDE trial for Ozempic in PAD. Ozempic showed 13% increase in max walking distance and gained 39.9 meters at ACC. Novo Nordisk to acquire obesity drug for $2 billion.
news.summary: Novo Nordisk A/S revealed the findings from the Phase 3b STRIDE trial, focused on peripheral artery disease (PAD), at the American College of Cardiology’s Annual Scientific Session and Expo. The study investigated Ozempic (semaglutide 1.0 mg), administered weekly, in individuals with type 2 diabetes and PAD. Results showed a notable 13% enhancement in maximum walking distance and a 39.9 meters mean treatment difference on a steep incline compared to placebo at the 52-week mark, meeting the primary goal. Additionally, semaglutide 1.0 mg outperformed placebo in all other secondary outcomes evaluated. Novo Nordisk is also set to acquire an obesity drug from a China-based biotech firm for approximately $2 billion.

news.headline: Netcompany and SDC merger receives regulatory approvals, significant milestone for the company.
Labor groups facing challenges after DOL cancels international programs amid Trump administration cuts.
news.summary: The Labor Department, as part of the Trump administration's budget cuts, has reduced funding for international labor rights efforts. This decision has left experts in this field feeling uncertain about the future.

news.headline: Garrett & Rohde & Schwarz collaborate on advanced security screening solution, combining metal detector with R&S QPS201 scanner for high-risk environments like prisons & precious metals security.
news.summary: Garrett has successfully integrated its walk-through metal detector with the Rohde & Schwarz' R&S QPS201 security scanner. The unified system offers advanced security features, making it a perfect choice for high-risk environments such as prisons or for preventing theft in the precious metals sector. By utilizing state-of-the-art millimeter-wave (mmWave) technology, the combined solution provides enhanced safety measures. This collaboration has resulted in a comprehensive security setup that caters to the specific needs of such critical settings.

news.headline: MiniTool launches Video Converter 4.0 with fresh interface and optimized features, significant update.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on April 2, 2025, MiniTool Software Limited launched MiniTool Video Converter 4.0, featuring a revamped user interface and enhanced functionalities. The latest version brings significant improvements to the software, catering to the growing needs of users. Notable upgrades in MiniTool Video Converter 4.0 include...

news.headline: Bitcoin awaits Trump tariff decision, Tesla down, Novo Nordisk earns $9B from weight loss drug sales. Crypto, US markets, politics and economy updates.
news.summary: The news article covers various aspects of the cryptocurrency market, US markets, politics, and economy. Peter Schiff criticizes Bitcoin as a 'digital risk' amid tariff fears. Circle's revenue is growing, but IPO filing shows falling profitability. Dogecoin and Shiba Inu are down over 15% in the past week. Experts predict short-term optimism for Bitcoin amidst Trump tariff announcement. A Trump family-backed Bitcoin mining firm may go public. DeepSeek's debut caused Bitcoin to drop below $100,000, but it may rise to $200,000 by year-end. Trump's travel ban may affect a Bitcoin-friendly country. Ethereum could drop to $1,500 before an upgrade, with $8,000 in sight. Circle, issuer of the world’s second-largest stablecoin, files for an IPO supported by Trump. Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Dogecoin edge higher as the market prepares for Trump's 'Liberation Day,' with Arthur Hayes predicting BTC hitting $250,000 by year-end. In the US markets, stock futures slip ahead of Tariffs announcement, with S&P 500 settling higher. DOJ advisor and ex-Elon Musk employee are suspected of past cybercrimes, and Elon Musk's DOGE and tightening expenses pose challenges for Palantir. Private employment beats expectations in the US.

news.headline: Coreweave achieves a new record-breaking AI inferencing benchmark with Nvidia GB200 Grace Blackwell superchips. $NVDA

Bitcoin hits $86k amid US tariff concerns, risking 11% dip before "Liberation Day." Markets await Trump's tariff announcement.BTC/USD at best since March 28. Volatility persists; QCP warns $76k BTC may return. Swissblock sees potential 11% drop.
news.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) hit new April highs at $86,444 on April 2, before US President Trump announced trade tariffs. Despite US stocks trading down, Bitcoin recovered, showing volatility as it reacted to news. Traders analyzed moving averages and trendlines, with some predicting a breakout towards $87,650. QCP Capital expressed skepticism, warning of pressure on risk assets post-tariff announcement. Despite some caution, asset management firm Swissblock remained positive on Bitcoin’s resilience, suggesting a potential drop to $76,000 if sentiments turned negative. This article emphasizes the risks involved in investment decisions and recommends conducting thorough research before making any moves.

news.headline: Activate ranks #1 in Leisure and Entertainment on The Financial Times list of Fastest-Growing Companies in Americas 2025, 12th overall.
news.summary: In Winnipeg, Manitoba on April 2, 2025, Activate has achieved the top position in Leisure and Entertainment on the 2025 Financial Times list of The Americas' Fastest Growing Companies. Across all industries, Activate was ranked 12th, marking a significant accomplishment. This recognition was granted in collaboration with a data provider and highlights Activate's exceptional growth and success in the market.

news.headline: CoreWeave sets new industry benchmark in AI inference with NVIDIA GB200 Grace Blackwell superchips. #MLPerf5.0 #RecordBreaking
news.summary: In a groundbreaking achievement, CoreWeave has emerged as the pioneer cloud service provider to present MLPerf Inference v5.0 results for NVIDIA GB200 Superchips. The company, based in Livingston, N.J., disclosed this remarkable feat on April 2, 2025. CoreWeave, recognized as the AI Hyperscaler™, has established a new standard in AI inference by showcasing its stellar performance in the MLPerf v5.0 with unprecedented numbers.

news.headline: RYZE Claim Solutions acquires Leading Edge Claims of Texas, expanding national presence. Headquarters in Florida & Texas.
news.summary: RYZE Claim Solutions, a national claims firm based in Florida, recently announced the acquisition of Leading Edge Claims, a Texas-based company. This acquisition expands RYZE's reach and services, combining the strengths of the two firms. The headquarters of RYZE Claim Solutions remains in Florida, with the acquired company based in Texas. This move signifies growth and strategic expansion for RYZE Claim Solutions in the claims industry.

news.headline: Chris Carrillo named as new CEO of North Texas Real Estate Information Systems, set to lead growth and innovation.
news.summary: A dynamic new leader has been appointed in Dallas to spearhead the next phase of growth and innovation for NTREIS. The appointment aims to drive significant progress and development for the organization.

news.headline: Oliveda International's subsidiary, Olive Tree People Inc., saw over 400% growth in Q1 2025 with sales exceeding $104.5 million.
news.summary: In Santa Monica, CA, on April 2, 2025, Oliveda International, Inc. shared that in the United States, Olive Tree People had a remarkable second year with sales exceeding $104.5 million. The company reported a growth of over 400% in the first quarter of 2025.

news.headline: James Barlow from BZI® honored as Top CEO of the Year 2025 by Utah Business and Cedar City Chamber of Commerce.
news.summary: James Barlow of BZI® in Kanarraville, Utah has been recognized as one of the top CEOs of the year 2025 by Utah Business. He has also been named CEO of the Year by Cedar City Chamber of Commerce. This prestigious acknowledgement highlights Barlow's exceptional leadership in the automotive industry.

news.headline: Omega Healthcare Investors, Inc. will release Q1 earnings on May 1st, 2025, with a conference call on May 2nd at 10am ET.
news.summary: Omega Healthcare Investors, Inc. (NYSE:OHI) from Hunt Valley, Maryland, shared plans to unveil its earnings report for the quarter concluding on March 31, 2025, post-market close on Thursday, May 1, 2025. The company will also host a conference call on Friday, May 2, 2025, at 10 a.m. Eastern Time to discuss its first-quarter 2025 results and recent updates. To participate in the webcast of the call, individuals can connect through www.omegahealthcare.com.

news.headline: Under Canvas launches Outdoor Collection brand, offering uniquely local properties for nature-based stays and experiences. #Denver #PRNewswire #April2 #2025
news.summary: On April 2, 2025, in Denver, Under Canvas, Inc., a prominent upscale outdoor hospitality company, unveiled its latest venture: the Outdoor Collection by Under Canvas. This new line features a series of properties providing nature-inspired accommodations and activities designed with careful thought and intention. The initial launch includes two properties as part of this collection, ushering in a unique experience for guests seeking immersive outdoor adventures.

news.headline: Grayscale debuts two new Bitcoin ETFs - BTCC & BPI. Aim to generate revenue via BTC volatility. 28 crypto products listed.
news.summary: Grayscale Investments unveiled two new Bitcoin-focused ETFs recently - the Grayscale Bitcoin Covered Call ETF (BTCC) and the Grayscale Bitcoin Premium Income ETF (BPI). These products aim to tap into Bitcoin's volatility for generating revenue that is less correlated to traditional investments. The Covered Call ETF focuses on maximizing income through writing calls close to spot prices, while the Premium Income ETF balances upside potential with income generation by targeting out-of-the-money call options. Grayscale promises differentiated revenue sources, monthly distributions, and options management for both products. Additionally, Grayscale filed for an ETF holding a mix of cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP, Solana, and Cardano. Nasdaq also filed to list Grayscale's Avalanche ETF recently. Grayscale is awaiting approval for XRP spot ETF, Cardano ETF, and Litecoin Trust conversion to an ETF. In total, Grayscale offers 28 crypto products.

news.headline: Elon Musk's plan to integrate blockchain in US Treasury raises both promise and challenges for government efficiency.
news.summary: Elon Musk's proposal to incorporate blockchain technology into the US Treasury has sparked a global debate on its potential to address government spending inefficiencies. The technology offers transparent audit trails and real-time tracking of payments, resources, and credentials. However, implementing blockchain at the government level poses challenges due to the scale of operations and the need for retraining government employees to adapt to modern infrastructure. Despite its potential for accountability and efficiency, concerns remain about data security and the complexity of transitioning from legacy systems. Musk's vision could lead to a more accountable system, but it also raises questions about privacy and governance in society. The article highlights the need for careful consideration of blockchain's role in shaping the future of governance.

news.headline: JM Test Systems acquires Alabama Scale & Instrument, Inc., expanding testing and calibration capabilities in the Southeast.
news.summary: JM Test Systems, a reputable company offering test equipment solutions and calibration services, has recently finalized the acquisition of Alabama Scale & Instrument, Inc. (ASI) based in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. The move is part of JM Test’s expansion strategy in the Southeastern region and aims to bolster their capabilities in test equipment services. This acquisition comes on the heels of JM Test’s purchase of BHD Instrumentation, demonstrating the company's commitment to simplifying test equipment processes for their clients. The acquisition of ASI is a significant step in JM Test’s growth, solidifying their presence in the market.

news.headline: Study shows shingles vaccine may reduce dementia risk, linking viral infection prevention to cognitive health.
news.summary: Research indicates that taking measures to avoid contracting the viral infection may assist in delaying cognitive decline.

news.headline: University Lab Partners selects nine MedTech companies for the I-CREATE Accelerator Program in Irvine, CA.
news.summary: In Irvine, California, on April 2, 2025, University Lab Partners (ULP) has revealed the names of nine innovative MedTech firms chosen to participate in the first group of the International Consortium for Research, Engineering, Acceleration of Technology Excellence (I-CREATE).

news.headline: Only 18% of companies effectively communicate the impact of employer branding, despite 78% investing in it.
news.summary: In New York on April 2, 2025, it was reported that despite a weakening job market, businesses are still prioritizing the recruitment of top talent to stay competitive. According to a recent study by The Conference Board, 78% of companies surveyed are focusing on employer branding strategies. This process is crucial for organizations to attract skilled professionals and retain a competitive advantage in the industry.

news.headline: Kaleidescape launches new Strato M movie player for residential, marine, and commercial use, priced at half the cost.
news.summary: New entry-level movie player by Kaleidescape has been launched in MOUNTAIN VIEW, Calif., on April 2, 2025. The Strato M is designed for residential, marine, and commercial theatre systems. Priced at a discount of 50%, the player offers a more accessible option for customers.
